title: Replace deprecated `PyObject_HasAttrString()` in inplace operators with exception-safe alternative
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

The C extension's 13 inplace numeric operator functions (`+=`, `-=`, `*=`, etc.) on `ObjectProxy` use `PyObject_HasAttrString()` to check whether the wrapped object supports the corresponding dunder method (e.g. `__iadd__`). This C-API function silently swallows **all** exceptions, not just `AttributeError`, meaning exceptions such as `KeyboardInterrupt`, `SystemExit`, or user exceptions raised from `__getattr__`/`__getattribute__` on the wrapped object are discarded. The code then falls through to the non-inplace branch as if the attribute didn't exist.

This is a semantic mismatch with the pure-Python implementation, which uses `hasattr()` — and `hasattr()` has only suppressed `AttributeError` since Python 3.2. `PyObject_HasAttrString()` is also deprecated as of Python 3.13 for exactly this reason.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/34c9fad09694958b83fbbea19ea30e69
